meaning: n.[植] 叶黄素,黄体制剂
examples: Eggs contain cysteine, sulfur, lecithin, amino acids and lutein.
Lutein is one that's a bright yellow. And lycopene is the carotenoid that makes tomatoes red.
Vegetables such as spinach and broccoli are high in lutein, which keeps your vision sharp and clear.
Dr. Chew said the AREDS study researchers recommend replacing the beta-carotene in supplements with lutein and zeaxanthin.
The study found a similar beneficial effect for people with cataracts who don't normally get a lot of lutein and zeaxanthin in their diet.
